content.thinking:
Now I understand the setup:
- There are 3 IngressClass resources: `ingress-1`, `ingress-2`, and `nginx`
- The demo Ingress `demo-app` currently uses `ingressClassName: nginx` and serves `demo.example.com`
- I need to create a new Ingress (or update one) that serves `class.example.com`

The task says the demo Ingress has "no ingress class" but I can see it does have `ingressClassName: nginx`. Let me check the test more carefully.

The test request is:
```bash
kubectl -n demo exec curl-test -- curl -sS -H "Host: class.example.com" \
http://ingress-gateway.demo.svc.cluster.local/
```

So the request goes to `ingress-gateway` service in the demo namespace with the Host header set to `class.example.com`. I need to figure out what `ingress-gateway` is and how it relates to the IngressClass resources.

(ingress-1 and ingress-2). The demo Ingress currently has no ingress
class and is not being picked up.

Your task: update the demo Ingress so requests to class.example.com
succeed.

```bash
  http://ingress-gateway.demo.svc.cluster.local/
```
